name: Mattia Niccoli
heroSub: I work at the intersection of geography, data, and politics — turning electoral results into spatial narratives at the finest granularity: the single sezione elettorale.
aboutTexts: I'm Mattia Niccoli, a researcher and analyst based in Florence. I work with electoral and spatial data, combining GIS tools with statistical analysis to understand how political behaviour varies across urban and suburban territories.

My current focus is on Florence and Sesto Fiorentino, where I am building a granular database of voting results at the sezione elettorale level across multiple elections — from the 2024 Comunali to the 2025 Regionali.
I work primarily in R for data processing and spatial joins, QGIS and ArcGIS Pro for cartography, and open-web tools for interactive publishing.
contactDesc: Interested in collaboration, data, or just want to discuss electoral geography? I'm always happy to connect.
footerText: © 2025 Mattia Niccoli — Built with GitHub Pages

projects.title: Voting Patterns in Florence & Sesto Fiorentino
projects.desc: Interactive maps at sezione elettorale level showing results, percentage shifts, and turnout across Comunali 2024 and Regionali 2025.

projects.title: Reconstructing Electoral Boundaries from Addresses
projects.desc: Automating the geocoding of 6,000+ addresses to reconstruct the sezioni boundaries for Sesto Fiorentino from scratch.

projects.title: Left-Right Shifts Across Tuscany
projects.desc: A regional overview of political realignment between 2020 and 2025, with a focus on swing sezioni and turnout collapse.
